verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain (confirmed: 'IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain') and is predicted to be a soluble, intra-acrosomal protein in sperm. Its loss after the acrosome reaction and failure to appear in head/tail fractions post-sonication support an intra-acrosomal (vesicular lumen) localization rather than plasma membrane association. The NCBI summary places it in the nucleus. Human Protein Atlas shows cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells. Contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence IZUMO4 reaches the outer PM leaflet under any stress or activation state; its loss post-AR argues against PM translocation unlike IZUMO1; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — even in sperm/testis, IZUMO4 is intra-acrosomal, not on the plasma membrane surface; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no evidence of lysosomal trafficking; (4) dual_localization — no documented PM pool; cytoplasmic/nuclear/acrosomal only; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no TM anchor, not wash-resistantly bound to any surface protein. No therapeutic programs targeting IZUMO4 on the cell surface found.
